characters.name: Elizabeth Bennet
characters.notes: Protagonist. Spirited, intelligent, witty. Refuses to marry without love.

characters.name: Mr. Fitzwilliam Darcy
characters.notes: Proud gentleman. Initially seems arrogant, but kind and honorable beneath the surface.

characters.name: Jane Bennet
characters.notes: Elizabeth's eldest sister. Sweet, gentle, in love with Mr. Bingley.

characters.name: Mr. Charles Bingley
characters.notes: Darcy's friend. Charming, good-natured, wealthy. Falls for Jane.

characters.name: Mr. Wickham
characters.notes: Charming but deceitful. Has history with Darcy. Pursues Elizabeth, then Lydia.

characters.name: Lydia Bennet
characters.notes: Youngest Bennet. Frivolous, wild, elopes with Wickham.

characters.name: Mary Bennet
characters.notes: Middle sister. Serious, pedantic, interested in books and morality.

characters.name: Kitty Bennet
characters.notes: Fourth Bennet sister. Easily influenced by Lydia.

characters.name: Mr. Bennet
characters.notes: Elizabeth's father. Witty but detached from family matters.

characters.name: Mrs. Bennet
characters.notes: Elizabeth's mother. Obsessed with marrying off her daughters.

characters.name: Lady Catherine de Bourgh
characters.notes: Darcy's aunt. Domineering, snobbish, opposes Elizabeth and Darcy's union.

characters.name: Anne de Bourgh
characters.notes: Lady Catherine's sickly daughter. Expected to marry Darcy.

characters.name: Charlotte Lucas
characters.notes: Elizabeth's best friend. Pragmatic, marries Mr. Collins for security.

characters.name: Mr. Collins
characters.notes: Obsequious clergyman. Marries Charlotte, fawns over Lady Catherine.

locations.name: Longbourn
locations.notes: Bennet family estate in Meryton. Elizabeth's home.

locations.name: Netherfield Park
locations.notes: Bingley's rented estate. Where Jane and Bingley's romance begins.

locations.name: Pemberley
locations.notes: Darcy's grand estate in Derbyshire. Symbol of his wealth and character.

locations.name: Rosings Park
locations.notes: Lady Catherine's estate in Kent. Where Darcy proposes to Elizabeth.

locations.name: Brighton
locations.notes: Seaside town. Where militia is stationed, where Lydia elopes.

locations.name: Meryton
locations.notes: Market town near Longbourn. Social hub for the Bennet family.

locations.name: London
locations.notes: Where Wickham and Lydia elope to. Where Darcy and Bingley reside.

locations.name: Hunsford
locations.notes: Village where Mr. Collins's parsonage is located, near Rosings.

themes.name: Pride and Prejudice
themes.notes: Darcy's pride, Elizabeth's prejudice. Both must overcome their flaws.

themes.name: Marriage and Social Status
themes.notes: Central theme. Women's limited options, economic necessity, love vs. security.

themes.name: First Impressions
themes.notes: Misjudgments based on appearance and initial behavior.

themes.name: Family Reputation
themes.notes: Lydia's elopement threatens the entire family's social standing.

themes.name: Love vs. Practicality
themes.notes: Charlotte's practical marriage vs. Elizabeth's romantic ideals.

themes.name: Self-Awareness
themes.notes: Character growth through recognizing one's own flaws.

themes.name: Class and Social Hierarchy
themes.notes: Darcy's arrogance about rank. Lady Catherine's snobbery.

misc.name: The Letter
misc.notes: Darcy's letter explaining his actions. Changes Elizabeth's mind.

misc.name: The Ball at Netherfield
misc.notes: Turning point. Darcy's first proposal, Bingley and Jane's connection.

misc.name: Lydia's Elopement
misc.notes: Crisis point. Threatens family honor. Darcy secretly pays for their marriage.

misc.name: The Tour of Pemberley
misc.notes: Elizabeth's visit changes her perception of Darcy.

scenes.title: The Bennet Family Receives News of Bingley's Arrival
scenes.summary: Mrs. Bennet learns that Mr. Bingley has rented Netherfield Park. She is determined to marry one of her daughters to him.
scenes.notes: Establishes the stakes: the Bennet family needs advantageous marriages.

scenes.title: First Meeting at Meryton
scenes.summary: The Bennet sisters encounter Mr. Bingley and Mr. Darcy in Meryton. Bingley is charming; Darcy appears proud and dismissive.
scenes.notes: Elizabeth forms negative first impression of Darcy. Meets Wickham.

scenes.title: The Ball at Netherfield
scenes.summary: A grand ball at Netherfield. Bingley dances with Jane. Darcy insults Elizabeth but then asks her to dance.
scenes.notes: Tension between Darcy and Elizabeth intensifies.

scenes.title: Elizabeth Visits Sick Jane
scenes.summary: Jane catches cold and stays at Netherfield. Elizabeth walks through muddy fields to visit her. Darcy observes Elizabeth's devotion.
scenes.notes: Darcy begins to see Elizabeth's good character despite her lower social status.

scenes.title: Mr. Collins Arrives
scenes.summary: The ridiculous Mr. Collins, the Bennet family's cousin and clergyman, arrives to stay. He is obsequious and dull.
scenes.notes: Comic relief. Establishes the family's weak social position.

scenes.title: Wickham's Story
scenes.summary: Wickham tells Elizabeth a tale of being wronged by Darcy, ingratiating himself with her. Elizabeth believes him.
scenes.notes: Elizabeth's prejudice against Darcy is reinforced. (Later revealed to be false.)

scenes.title: Charlotte Accepts Mr. Collins
scenes.summary: Charlotte Lucas pragmatically agrees to marry Mr. Collins for security and status, shocking Elizabeth.
scenes.notes: Contrasts Elizabeth's romantic ideals with Charlotte's practical concerns.

scenes.title: Bingley's Departure
scenes.summary: Bingley abruptly leaves Netherfield, breaking Jane's heart. Darcy's involvement is suspected but unclear.

scenes.title: Elizabeth Visits Rosings with Charlotte
scenes.summary: Elizabeth stays with Charlotte and Mr. Collins at the parsonage near Rosings. She meets Lady Catherine de Bourgh.
scenes.notes: Lady Catherine is imperious and snobbish. Hints at her expectation that Darcy will marry Anne.

scenes.title: Darcy's First Proposal
scenes.summary: Darcy, tortured by his feelings, proposes to Elizabeth in an insulting manner. Elizabeth furiously refuses him.
scenes.notes: Turning point. Darcy lists reasons why marrying Elizabeth is beneath him.

scenes.title: Darcy's Letter
scenes.summary: Darcy gives Elizabeth a letter explaining his actions regarding Bingley and Wickham. Elizabeth's prejudice begins to crack.
scenes.notes: Elizabeth learns that Wickham is a fortune-hunter and seducer. Darcy separated Bingley from Jane to protect him.

scenes.title: Elizabeth Tours Pemberley
scenes.summary: Elizabeth tours Darcy's magnificent estate, Pemberley, while he is away. Her respect for him grows. Then Darcy returns unexpectedly.
scenes.notes: Darcy is gracious and kind, contradicting Elizabeth's prejudice.

scenes.title: Lydia Elopes with Wickham
scenes.summary: Lydia runs away to London with the unprincipled Wickham. The Bennet family's reputation is in grave danger.
scenes.notes: Crisis. A single woman eloping ruins the family's prospects for good marriages.

scenes.title: Darcy Secretly Pays for Lydia's Marriage
scenes.summary: Darcy, unknown to Elizabeth, pays Wickham to marry Lydia, saving the Bennet family's reputation.
scenes.notes: Darcy's true character is revealed. He acts honorably without seeking recognition.

scenes.title: Bingley Returns and Proposes to Jane
scenes.summary: Bingley returns to Netherfield and proposes to Jane. She accepts happily.

scenes.title: Lady Catherine Opposes Darcy and Elizabeth
scenes.summary: Lady Catherine learns of the possibility of Darcy marrying Elizabeth and vehemently opposes it, commanding Elizabeth to refuse him.
scenes.notes: Elizabeth refuses to be intimidated by Lady Catherine's arrogance.

scenes.title: Darcy's Second Proposal
scenes.summary: Darcy proposes again, this time humbly and respectfully. Elizabeth, having overcome her prejudice, accepts him joyfully.
scenes.notes: Happy ending. Both characters have grown and overcome their flaws.

scenes.title: Epilogue: Marriages and Happiness
scenes.summary: The novel concludes with Elizabeth and Darcy's engagement, Jane and Bingley's happiness, and the promise of future contentment.
scenes.notes: Resolution. The heroines have secured good marriages for love and security.
